name: Standing float serve (literature)
description: Reference values from kinematic studies; side view. Contact = head to hand; elbow = head to elbow at hit.
citation: Spatio-temporal serve studies; typical standing float. Elbow 112-117°, shoulder 114-121° at contact (Cal State float serve thesis).

name: Jump float serve (literature)
description: Reference values from kinematic studies; side view. MHTC and elbow timing from high-level players.
citation: Kinematic analyses (MHTC, elbow timing); elite male players. Float effect greater in vertical plane (Frontiers 2020).

name: Jump float serve (Khan et al. IJEDR 2019)
description: 15 Indian international male players (mean height 1.96m). MHTC 312±47cm, arm cocking/acceleration phase.
citation: Khan, Hussain, Ahmad. IJEDR 2019. MHTC 312±47cm, SSHN 280±15cm. EEAN and MHTC differed significantly between float vs topspin.

name: Jump topspin serve (literature)
description: Reference values from kinematic studies; side view. Higher contact than float; approach velocity key predictor.
citation: Spatio-temporal analysis; international players. Elbow angle at contact 119-133° (Bari et al. Medicine 2023).

name: Jump topspin serve (Bari et al. 2023)
description: U-23 collegiate men (n=10, height 182.6±4cm). Spike height 327.4±3.8cm; elbow 133°, shoulder 71° at contact.
citation: Bari et al. Medicine (Baltimore) 2023. 30 male collegiate players, 3 cameras 120fps. Approach velocity, SEACP, CG height predict ball velocity.

name: Jump float serve (Bari et al. 2023)
description: U-21/U-19 collegiate players. Lower spike height than topspin; similar elbow extension at contact.
citation: Bari et al. Medicine 2023. U-21 spike 268.8±24.7cm, U-19 spike 287.6±30.2cm. Elbow angle 119-128° at contact.
